channel.title: Hands-on Amazon GameLift :: Hands-on Amazon GameLift: Deploy Your First Multiplayer Game
channel.description: Deploy Your First Multiplayer Game Welcome! In roughly 2 hours you will deploy and operate a complete multiplayer online game on AWS — a retro pixel-art racing game called Pixel Rush — and use it to learn the core capabilities of Amazon GameLift Servers: What a dedicated game server is and why real-time multiplayer needs one The GameLift Server SDK lifecycle that every hosted game implements GameLift Anywhere — register your own machine as fleet compute for fast iteration Managed EC2 fleets — production hosting with builds, runtime configs and queues FlexMatch — rule-based matchmaking, tickets and event notifications At the end you will race against other participants on a shared arena, with every piece of the pipeline — matchmaking, session placement, realtime state — running on infrastructure you deployed yourself.

item.title: 1. Introduction
item.description: Duration: ~10 minutes (reading only) Before touching the console, let’s build the mental model: why multiplayer games need dedicated servers, and which GameLift component solves which problem.

item.title: 2. Setup
item.description: Duration: ~20 minutes Two paths — pick the one that matches how you’re attending: At an AWS event → a temporary account and a browser IDE are provided (2.1) In your own account → install a few tools locally (2.2) Then everyone clones the repo, bootstraps CDK, and deploys the game backend.

item.title: 3. GameLift Anywhere
item.description: Duration: ~25 minutes Your first game session — hosted on your own machine, orchestrated by GameLift. You’ll learn the Anywhere concept, walk through the Server SDK code, then run it.

item.title: 4. Managed Fleet
item.description: Duration: ~25 minutes (including ~15 min fleet activation — used for reading) Time for production hosting: upload your server build and let GameLift run it on managed EC2 instances.

item.title: 5. FlexMatch
item.description: Duration: ~20 minutes The last missing piece: how players find each other. Rule sets, tickets, and the event pipeline — then you’ll point matchmaking at your new EC2 fleet and race.

item.title: 6. Race Day
item.description: Duration: ~15 minutes Everything is deployed. Two final experiences: prove your own stack end-to-end, then join everyone on the shared arena.

item.title: Appendix: Multi-Region (Optional)
item.description: Duration: ~20 minutes · requires Modules 1–5 completed One fleet, three continents — serve players from the region closest to them.
